methods.method_name: Ice Core Data Collection and Description
methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing ice core samples for isotope analysis using a Picarro Inc. cavity ring-down spectroscopy (CRDS) instrument, coupled to a continuous flow analysis (CFA) system. This method is used to determine isotopic ratios as proxies for local temperature and regional atmospheric circulation.

steps.description: The ratios of 2 H/ 1 H and 18 O/ 16 O from the West Antarctic Ice Sheet Divide core (WDC) were analyzed using a Picarro Inc. cavity ring-down spectroscopy (CRDS) instrument, coupled to a continuous flow analysis (CFA) system.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze isotopic ratios using CRDS-CFA system
inputs: West Antarctic Ice Sheet Divide core samples

steps.description: The data are reported in delta (δ) notation relative to Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (VSMOW), normalized to the Standard Light Antarctic Water (SLAP) scale.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize isotopic data to VSMOW and SLAP scales

steps.description: Water-isotope data measured on the Picarro instrument were recorded at a rate of 1.18 Hz (0.85 s intervals). Ice samples were moved through the CRDS-CFA system at a rate of 2.5 cm/min, yielding millimeter resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: Record water-isotope data at specified intervals and resolution

steps.description: The data were then averaged over non-overlapping 0.5 cm bins.
For each of these data points, an age was determined using the WDC depth-age scale, providing climate data from 0-68 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine age for data points using WDC depth-age scale

steps.description: Annual dating of this record extends to 31 ka, with the remainder relying on tie points to the Hulu Cave timescale.
steps.searchable_summary: Extend dating using Hulu Cave timescale
